meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Code Fast: A Freelancer’s Workflow Guide
content.seo_description: Master professional debugging workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code. Learn to ship faster and impress clients with this intermediate-level masterclass.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracing logs. To resolve stubborn bugs, isolate the failure point using Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for iterative hot-patching. By standardizing your diagnostic workflow, you eliminate guesswork, ensure code integrity, and restore client confidence in your ability to ship production-ready SaaS applications on schedule.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your terminal is bleeding red errors. You aren't just stuck; you're losing billable hours while staring at a logic loop that refuses to yield. The difference between a junior developer and a senior freelancer isn't the absence of bugs—it's the speed at which they dismantle them.

We are moving beyond basic console logs. This workflow leverages high-speed AI tools to map complexity and execute surgical fixes. By treating your codebase as a system to be analyzed rather than a puzzle to be guessed, you reclaim control, protect your margins, and deliver the flawless performance your clients pay for.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Surface
steps.description: Identify the exact module responsible for the crash to prevent scope creep during debugging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current directory and map the dependency graph for the failing module. Identify the entry point of the error trace.

steps.title: Generate a Diagnostic Hypothesis
steps.description: Use AI to hypothesize the root cause based on current stack traces, saving hours of manual investigation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Given the error logs provided, hypothesize three potential failure points. Rank them by likelihood and suggest a test case for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze these logs and suggest the most likely culprit for the runtime crash.'

steps.title: Create a Reproducible Test Harness
steps.description: Ensure the bug is captured in a test so you can verify the fix instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a minimal integration test that triggers the specific error identified in step 2. Use Jest syntax.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit test/debug-harness.test.ts --prompt 'Create a test case that reproduces the current error.'

steps.title: Execute Surgical Hot-Patching
steps.description: Apply the fix iteratively while maintaining the integrity of the surrounding architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for the identified module that resolves the race condition while maintaining existing interface signatures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/modules/fix-me.ts --prompt 'Apply the architectural fix suggested by the analysis.'

steps.title: Validate and Regression Check
steps.description: Verify the fix doesn't break dependent services before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the applied patch against the codebase. Are there any downstream side effects or performance regressions?

pro_tips: Always commit the failing test case before applying the fix to demonstrate the 'before and after' to your client.
Use Claude Code's 'review' flag on your own PRs to catch logic gaps before they reach the staging environment.
Antigravity is best used for high-level structure; don't rely on it for deep-dive syntax corrections—use it to map, then use Claude to patch.
If a bug is intermittent, wrap your diagnostics in a 'flaky test' detector to catch execution timing issues.
Always document the 'why' of the fix in the code comments; your future self and your clients will value the technical transparency.
content.next_steps: Integrate this diagnostic sequence into your CI/CD pipeline as a pre-commit hook to catch recurring issues before they impact your production environment.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your development velocity. Implement this workflow today to transform your debugging from a bottleneck into a competitive advantage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
